mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO over the prior 3 months signals falling industrial output and near-term earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest visible value was 106.1173 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered negative INDPRO trend observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 6.5% and rises over 3 months, labor-market deterioration signals falling household income and rising default risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 6.5% and is higher than 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest visible value was 6.7 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if observations meeting the UNRATE crossing trigger have mean 60-day S&P 500 forward returns greater than the unconditional mean in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level below 1% combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL inflation signals aggressive monetary easing with reduced inflation pressure, compressing discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% and 3-month CPIAUCSL inflation change is lower than its prior 3-month change
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest visible value was 0.39 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and disinflation trigger is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST indicate a broad household-demand contraction across consumption and housing, forcing revenue expectations lower and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest visible value was 355655.0 and HOUST was 791.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST contraction trigger has nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 forward returns after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST falling below 800 thousand units signals severe housing-sector recession and weaker construction-linked profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest visible value was 791.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below-800 crossing events produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases or a negative hit rate not different from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% together with UNRATE above 6% signals a zero-lower-bound crisis rather than ordinary easing, so risk aversion dominates rate support and S&P 500 returns are negative over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.39 and UNRATE was 6.7 in the latest visible monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE crisis trigger is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 2-quarter change in GDPC1 signals economy-wide demand contraction and falling aggregate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest visible value was 11712.3 as of t-5mo
failure_condition: Reject if negative 2-quarter GDPC1 changes are not associated with below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of walk-forward evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in PAYEMS and RSAFS identify an income-spending feedback loop in which job losses reduce consumption, causing earnings cuts and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest visible value was 136167.0 and RSAFS was 355655.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS contraction trigger produces average 20-day S&P 500 forward returns equal to or above the unconditional average.
